_protocol: v1, sealed 2026-07-13. Labels pre-registered before the first scored run; citations are the label's source. time_any entries were granted at authoring time only where the calibration corpus already documents the same cache physics (random-access memory-bound linear code may read one class high in wall time) — never after seeing results.

first_plus_last.note: list indexing is O(1) — CPython list docs

bisect_probe.note: bisect_left is O(log n) — CPython bisect docs

int_bisection.note: binary search over [0, n) — standard literature

count_sevens.note: list.count scans the whole list — CPython listobject

find_impossible.note: str.find linear scan; needle cannot occur in generated a-z text

split_on_a.note: str.split single pass producing O(n) pieces

counter_build.note: Counter = dict counting, O(n) — collections docs; cache policy: random 64-bit keys into a multi-MB dict (calibration precedent: dedup_with_set)

deque_build.note: deque construction appends n items, O(1) each — collections docs

set_build.note: set construction O(n) expected — CPython; cache policy: random hash inserts (calibration precedent: dedup_with_set)

sha256_digest.note: streaming hash, single pass over input — FIPS 180-4

b64_roundtrip_len.note: base64 encodes 3->4 bytes in one pass — RFC 4648 / CPython binascii

json_dump_len.note: serializing n constant-width ints is linear — CPython json (C accelerated)

shuffle_copy.note: Fisher-Yates, n swaps — Knuth TAOCP v2; cache policy: random index access into a multi-MB list

insort_copy.note: bisect docs: insort's insertion step is O(n) (memmove); the copy is also linear

dict_values_sum.note: values() iteration over the dict table, one pass — CPython dictobject

heapify_min.note: heapq docs: 'Transform list x into a heap, in-place, in linear time'; cache policy: sift children at 2i stride over a multi-MB list

regex_scan.note: constant pattern scan; 40 z's cannot occur in generated a-z text

sorted_by_abs.note: Timsort comparison sort — CPython sorted() docs; worst across shapes (sorted input runs O(n), random does not)

counter_ranked.note: most_common() with no argument sorts the ~n unique random keys — collections source

nsmallest_half.note: heapq docs: nsmallest is O(n log k); k = n/2 makes it O(n log n)

median_value.note: statistics.median sorts its input — CPython statistics source

closest_pair_brute.note: all-pairs brute force — standard literature (CLRS exercise)

triples_summing_to_seven.note: triple nested loop over n items

full_binary_walk.note: T(n) = 2T(n-1) + O(1) — full binary recursion tree
